checks.a_analysis: The output JSON follows the structure and content of the source PDF. No completely invented paragraphs or sentences were found. While there is a typo ('CowdStrike'), it is an error in transcription of existing text rather than a fabrication.
URLs and rates were verified. The URL 'https://www.symantec.com/services/education-services/training-courses' and the rate '$285/hr' for the Security Engineer match the source document perfectly.

The first table of the 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List' appearing on page 11 of the source PDF is entirely missing from the output JSON. This table contains items with line numbers 1 through 9.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table titled 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List' containing Line No. 1 to 9 (starting with PART NO. CWACDE-AB-HI).
checks.b_evidence_output: The output skips directly from the Trellix Professional Services text to 'Carahsoft Trellix Price List Items 10-29'.

checks.a_analysis: Several tables in the output JSON are severely truncated, containing only the first row of data despite the source tables having ten or more rows. Specifically: the Trellix table for items 10-29 only has one row (item 10); the Trellix table for items 30-43 only has row 30; the DLT CrowdStrike table on page 14 only has the first row; and the Presidio Cisco table on page 17 only has the first row.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 12: Table with 20 items (Line No. 10-29); Page 13: Table with 14 items (Line No. 30-43); Page 14: Table with 10 cost items; Page 17: Table with 10 cost items.
checks.b_evidence_output: Table 'Carahsoft Trellix Price List Items 10-29' has 1 row; Table 'Carahsoft Trellix Price List Items 30-43' has 1 data row; Table 'DLT CrowdStrike Cost Proposal Form' has 1 row; Table 'Presidio Cisco Cost Proposal Form' has 1 row.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for Trellix, CrowdStrike, and Cisco (Pages 12, 13, 14, 17)

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Large portions of the document are not correctly converted into structured text. The Sophos (pages 3-7) and SHI Trellix (pages 8-9) sections are represented solely as images, meaning all tabular text data is missing from the JSON stream. Additionally, the summary table on page 15 and the 'Notes' section on page 16 for CrowdStrike are completely omitted.
checks.b_evidence_source: Detailed pricing tables for Sophos (pages 3-7) and SHI Trellix (pages 8-9); Summary table on page 15; 'Notes' section on page 16.
checks.b_evidence_output: Sophos and SHI Trellix data provided as image elements only; Page 15 table and Page 16 notes are absent.

checks.a_analysis: A significant typo was introduced in a section heading where 'CrowdStrike' was spelled as 'CowdStrike'.

checks.a_analysis: The output document follows the linear progression of the source PDF. While headers like 'ENDPOINT RESELLER PRICE LIST' and page numbers appear multiple times in the source, their presence in the output corresponds to their respective locations in the source document. No significant instances of unauthorized text duplication (e.g., repeating paragraphs or table rows) were identified.

The heading hierarchy is generally maintained. The main document title is reflected in the metadata. Each major vendor section (Broadcom, Sophos, Trellix, CrowdStrike, Presidio) is assigned a section title. Within these, the sub-vendor lists are correctly nested as H3 headings. Some minor adjustments have been made (such as promoting page headers to headings), but these are consistent with accessibility normalization and do not break the logical flow or skip levels inappropriately.

There is a significant failure in table structure conversion across multiple sections of the document. Firstly, the tables for Sophos (Pages 5-9) and SHI Trellix (Pages 10-11) are captured as 'image' elements rather than structured 'table' elements, losing all accessible row/column data. Secondly, the Carahsoft Trellix table spanning Pages 13-15 is severely truncated in the JSON; for instance, the table labeled 'Items 10-29' contains only one data row (Item 10) and the table for 'Items 30-43' contains only Item 30, with items 1-9 and 11-29 being omitted entirely. Thirdly, the DLT CrowdStrike table on Page 17 of the source is completely missing from the output. Finally, the 'DLT CrowdStrike Extended Cost Details' table (heading-12) is rendered as an empty header row with no data cells. These issues represent a major failure to preserve the structural organization of the source tables.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains extensive tables for Sophos (pp. 5-9), SHI Trellix (pp. 10-11), a 43-line table for Carahsoft Trellix (pp. 13-15), and a continued table for CrowdStrike (p. 17).
checks.b_evidence_output: Sophos and SHI Trellix tables are images (e.g., image_count: 8). Carahsoft Trellix tables contain single rows (Items 10 and 30). The Page 17 table is omitted.

checks.a_analysis: Aside from the omission of specific content blocks (notably the table on page 17), the general information flow follows the logical sequence of the source document, progressing through vendor price lists in the same order as the PDF pages.

The list found under the 'Professional Services' section for Trellix (pages 11-12) has been correctly identified and converted into a structured list element in the JSON, preserving its meaning and numbering.

The output correctly captures the text of URLs found in the source (e.g., the Symantec training URL on page 3) without injecting additional or fabricated links. While the links are not hyperlinked in the JSON, the text accuracy is maintained.

checks.a_analysis: Several sections of the price list are missing their core content in the HTML output. Specifically, the entire Sophos price list (PDF pages 3-7) and SHI Trellix price list (PDF pages 8-9) are empty except for headings. Additionally, the first table of the Carahsoft Trellix section (lines 1-9 from PDF page 11) is completely missing from the HTML rendering. This fundamentally breaks the visual organization and intent of the document as a comprehensive price list.
checks.b_evidence_source: PDF pages 3-9 (Sophos/SHI Trellix lists) and page 11 (Carahsoft Trellix lines 1-9) contain primary pricing tables.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows headings for 'SHI (Sophos) Price List', 'SHI Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List', and 'Carahsoft Trellix (Formerly McAfee) Price List', but the content tables for Sophos and Trellix (SHI) are missing, and the Carahsoft Trellix section skips lines 1-9, starting at line 10.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections for Sophos, SHI Trellix, and Carahsoft Trellix.

checks.a_analysis: The tables that were successfully converted (e.g., Symantec, DLT CrowdStrike, and portions of Carahsoft Trellix) are rendered with clear boundaries, distinguishable rows/columns, and readable headers. While many tables are entirely missing (captured in V1/V3), the ones present are visually coherent.

The price list tables on PDF pages 3 through 9 appear to be embedded image blocks (scans or screenshots from other documents). These are meaningful content images that are essential to the document. None of these image-based tables are present in the HTML output, leaving those sections blank.
checks.b_evidence_source: Visual table blocks on PDF pages 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 9.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output contains only the headings for these pages with no image content or equivalent tables rendered.

checks.a_analysis: No instances of duplicated content blocks, paragraphs, or tables were observed in the HTML output.
